item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe my article got featured!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I'd like to thank my parents for giving me to the world, and my lack of creative writing ability
Was +1 number 100, congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fiddled around with the quote tags. I wish I could do nested quotes, but eventually I settled on quoting the whole letter to maintain the impression that it's just a piece of paper, plain text.

The fact that it plays out so well like that makes this a really good skip

It is, isn't it? It just never got its day in the limelight, which is a shame since people who actually read it seem to like it.

It also reads a bit weirdly that part of it is the description just slightly reworded

There are two versions of the description being actively maintained, both written by the same person: the CC2/1531 edition and the real info.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rolled my eyes so hard at the object initially, but as the interview logs went on I realized something was up. And then the ending letter cinched it. The fact that it plays out so well like that makes this a really good skip, though the ending letter really should be put in quote boxes to read better. It also reads a bit weirdly that part of it is the description just slightly reworded, instead of the researcher paraphrasing a description of their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this.

A 100% accurate1 lie detector.

Footnotes
1. for an internally consistent form of "accurate"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the purpose of readability, I threw around a lot of paragraphs here and there and rewrote the entire interview log in accordance with the Foundation standards for those. Hopefully it's less wall of text-y this time. Waiting for feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is yes on many levels. +1 sir, well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They ran every statement from the false description through the skip, and it deemed everything true.

Everything the infected says in accord with the output is infectious.

Therefore, everything the infected says about the skip is infectious.

You do have a point with the interview format, however. It needs some bolding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm neutral on this. The formatting is messy in a lot of places; the interviews would look better and be more readable with the standard interview template, and the last letter could be put in a quotebox and the paragraph breaks edited to match those in the description.

Note that "SCP-1531 is a polygraph that is impossible to deceive" and all other properties SCP-1531 is believed to possess are found true by affected individuals, and as such, any statement regarding the object is a vector of infection.

I'm not sure I understand how that works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done! I would have upvoted even without the final letter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought it was a bit dull at first, but test 117 is really good and the final note of course clinched it. I'd been hoping it was headed for a denouement like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! I started to understand what was really going on about halfway through the interview logs, which I think is really ideal for a piece like this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed direct mention of torture, as per consensus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this quite a bit. One minor nitpick though:

Under no circumstances make any inquires regarding 1531

It seems like that should be 'inquiries' rather than 'inquires.' Besides that, though, good work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It does not make people go insane or anything, just paranoia, suspension of disbelief, mental gymnastics here and there, lack of attention to detail…

Plus, they are frustrated that they have an artifact that can do great things and think they are losing a futile war against the bureaucracy that doesn't even let them acquire any new D-Class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like this, a lot. And I think the execution is perfect upon re-read.

Just weird dialogue lines like

The guy is under a memetic effect to believe anything the box says. Of course he's going to be acting a tiny bit weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have a point here [that I wish people would have expressed before], and on second thought the letter and the experiment logs would sound better if emotionally detached. The whole Steiner character is severely downplayed from the original draft and is no longer really the protagonist of the story. So some of it will leave. 'cept for the whole Maurice part. I like it. It implies things that I like.

Edit: I have actually rewritten the letter, kind of, but the logs were fine for the most part. I don't see the issue as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if he's all revved up like, say, a deuce?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like the concept, not as thrilled with the execution. Just weird dialogue lines like

Dr. Earhart: I think we already determined you don't feel any compulsion. 16891, are you trying to guilt trip us or what?

out of a Foundation researcher in the middle of an interrogation, in the middle of an article full of interrogation logs, throw me off. That and the entire Themis-1531 document is a slightly modified version of the Description section of the main article itself, but unformatted, so it sounds like it's a personal correspondence in clinical tone. It's like trying to seduce a woman using the terminology of the owner's manual of a 1992 Dodge Omni; the language works fine the first time you use it, but it isn't as appropriate the second time. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who leads Justice to the light? Not the person blinded by it.
